page: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton3 processor utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture. Running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, the processor provides 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the c7g.2xlarge in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1473.0 and a multi-core score of 8116.0, alongside a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 12238.41. Memory bandwidth is measured at 774.89 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 146.42 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database and web-serving simulations, the server managed 13.27 million Redis SET operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 15.45 GB/sec. However, certain workloads show limitations; the Passmark CPU score of 6057.47 and bzip3 decompression speeds are categorized as weak, while prompt processing for a 7B LLM model is poor at 14.13 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture provides a balanced compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2, making it a resource-dense option for scale-out workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The lack of bundled local storage and GPU hardware positions this instance as a dedicated compute node rather than a storage-heavy database or machine learning training server. It is highly suitable for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, containerized microservices, and general-purpose application hosting where dedicated physical cores and steady-state performance are prioritized over burstable capacity.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton3 processor. Operating at 2.6 GHz with a dedicated 1-thread-per-core configuration, it features 16.0 GB of system memory, a 32 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard operations, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8116.0, a static web serving throughput of 15.45 GB/sec, and 13.27 million Redis SET operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in complex decompression and large-model LLM prompt processing. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated compute workloads, making it well-suited for software compilation, web serving, and containerized applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, suitable for web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring eight dedicated physical cores and balanced memory bandwidth for steady-state application workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton3 ARM64 processor running at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB of L3 cache
Delivers 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero bundled local storage
Achieves 13.27 million Redis SET operations per second and 15.45 GB/sec web serving throughput
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and containerized microservices
